selected_references.caption: Self-Evolution Feedback Loop in Dr. Zero. The Proposer generates QA Pairs via Reason & Search, which the Solver attempts to solve. Predictions yield Difficulty Rewards and Outcome Rewards. Step 1 updates the Proposer via HRPO; Step 2 updates the Solver via GRPO.
selected_references.description: Self-Evolution Feedback Loop in Dr. Zero. The Proposer generates QA Pairs via Reason & Search, which the Solver attempts to solve. Predictions yield Difficulty Rewards and Outcome Rewards. Step 1 updates the Proposer via HRPO; Step 2 updates the Solver via GRPO.
selected_references.reason: This reference is an excellent match for the 'Agent & Reasoning' category, featuring a multi-step process with a 'Proposer' (analogous to LLM Inductor), a 'Solver', and a crucial 'Feedback Loop' (matching the '3-Gate Verifier'). Its structure aligns with a pipeline that involves intelligent components and iterative refinement.

selected_references.caption: Overview of StructMAE. (a) The overall pipeline: Input Graph -> SBS (Structure-based Scoring) -> SGM (Structure-guided Masking) -> Enc -> Dec -> Reconstructed Nodes. (b) SBS module with pre-defined/learnable scoring and TopK selection. (c) SGM module showing progressive masking across training epochs.
selected_references.description: Overview of StructMAE. (a) The overall pipeline: Input Graph -> SBS (Structure-based Scoring) -> SGM (Structure-guided Masking) -> Enc -> Dec -> Reconstructed Nodes. (b) SBS module with pre-defined/learnable scoring and TopK selection. (c) SGM module showing progressive masking across training epochs.
selected_references.reason: This reference provides a strong visual structural match for the user's request for a 'wide, flat horizontal pipeline diagram' with distinct modules and sub-modules. Its clean, academic style and clear flow are highly relevant for guiding the overall layout and aesthetic, despite being from a different primary category.
